qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: HTTP server started without ReadTimeout, ReadHeaderTimeout, or WriteTimeout enabling Slowloris denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns HTTP server timeout configuration in cmd/gogs/web.go, not release deletion tag-name option injection through internal/database/release.go.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GET-based state-changing endpoints bypass CSRF protection for organization and team operations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns CSRF on GET organization and team endpoints, not the advisory's Git command argument handling during release deletion.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mirror repository address update accepts arbitrary URLs without SSRF validation enabling internal network probing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns missing URL validation in mirror address updates and mirror sync, not rel.TagName reaching git tag -d without an end-of-options separator.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: rel.TagName passed to git tag -d during release deletion without -- or --end-of-options. The qualifying findings target unrelated code paths and vulnerability mechanisms.
evidence: The advisory affected component is internal/database/release.go, specifically process.ExecDir(..., "git", "tag", "-d", rel.TagName) without an end-of-options separator.
missing-http-server-timeouts targets cmd/gogs/web.go HTTP server timeout configuration.
csrf-get-state-change targets GET-based organization and team state-changing endpoints plus CSRF middleware behavior.
ssrf-mirror-address targets mirror address URL validation and mirror sync behavior.
A session artifact search for release.go, TagName, process.ExecDir, and git tag -d found no qualifying finding describing the advisory source-to-sink path.
